name: Studio annotation canvas (draw/edit regions)
story: As an annotator, I want to mark the exact regions that matter on an image, so that my observations are pinned to what I'm discussing.
expected: Annotorious renders the PixiJS overlay; drawing a rect creates a region; editing moves/resizes it; selection opens the editor.
notes: seed row — verify against fs-seam test backend in vite dev | GAP: apps/studio annotorious-draw e2e (no unit test for PixiJS overlay draw) | HUMAN-GATE Archie-79be: rendered-DOM/interaction, no e2e harness — data-logic covered by render-core, visual verdict deferred (not self-certified)

name: Viewer shared NotePopup (float-on-select + stepper)
story: As a reader, I want a note to surface when I select what it describes, so that I can read it in context.
expected: One shared NotePopup.svelte; floats on selection, stepper only when collapsed, flip-and-read.
notes: seed row — see project memory project_viewer_two_note_popups | GAP: NotePopup.svelte float/stepper component test | HUMAN-GATE Archie-79be: rendered-DOM/interaction, no e2e harness — data-logic covered by render-core, visual verdict deferred (not self-certified)

name: Published pages emit og-tags/JSON-LD/sitemap
story: As a publisher, I want my pages to preview and index well when shared, so that people can find and recognize my exhibit.
expected: pageShell() injects OpenGraph + Twitter meta + schema.org JSON-LD; sitemap.xml emitted (Q-8).
notes: seed row — assert on generated HTML, no network

name: Import remote IIIF manifest by URL
story: As a curator, I want to bring in images from an institutional IIIF source, so that I can annotate collections I don't have to host.
expected: iiif-import.ts fetches info.json + tiles; OpenSeadragon renders folios.
notes: seed row — use VENDORED fixture, not live Yale; timeout => flaky not fail

name: Native file-open dialog (Tauri fs-seam)
story: As a desktop user, I want to open local files through the system dialog, so that I can annotate images stored on my computer.
expected: @tauri-apps/plugin-dialog opens native picker; fs-seam reads bytes.
notes: seed row — native shell only; waived + smoke in CI (tauri build). Logic tested via fs-seam test backend.

name: Studio note create/edit/delete
story: As a curator, I want to capture, revise, and remove my observations, so that the exhibit always reflects my current thinking.
expected: Region or whole-object note creation; form commits comment/tags; delete confirms; edits persist on close.
edges: delete last note in a reading

name: Studio readings create/rename/recolor/delete
story: As a curator, I want competing interpretations kept as distinct layers, so that readers can weigh them against each other.
expected: New reading mints stable id; color updates all marks; rename keeps notes attached; removing reading keeps notes under General.
edges: duplicate reading names mint unique id
remove the active reading falls back to base

name: Studio filter notes by reading visibility
story: As a curator, I want to isolate or compare interpretations while I work, so that I can focus on one argument or contrast several.
expected: Toggling hides/shows reading notes; comparing 2+ triggers outline-only render; base always visible.

name: Studio citation picker (Cmd-K)
story: As a curator, I want to reference other notes and objects in my library, so that my argument stays connected to its evidence.
expected: Cmd-K opens search/browse modal; picking inserts encoded archie: ref at cursor.
edges: item with no thumbnail (AV label plate)

name: Studio author narrative sections/beats
story: As a curator, I want to give the exhibit a guided throughline, so that visitors can follow my argument in order.
expected: Section targets an object with prose + optional framed region; persists across object switches.
edges: adding first section flips front door + one-time cue
frame region then cancel does not capture

name: Studio annotate audio/video (temporal + spatiotemporal)
story: As a curator, I want to anchor observations to specific moments in audio and video, so that time-based evidence is as precise as image evidence.
expected: Waveform for audio; in/out marking (I key); video frame box (B); Space toggles play; arrows nav notes; autosave.
edges: draw on paused vs playing video

name: Studio add/frame geo-annotations
story: As a curator, I want to ground observations in real-world places, so that location becomes part of the evidence.
expected: Modal picks provider, sets bounds (drag or type), zoom, name; emits tileSource descriptor.
edges: switch provider mid-edit keeps valid bounds
notes: src AddMapModal.svelte; tile fetch is network but auth/bounds logic is browser

name: Studio edit exhibit title/description/rights
story: As a curator, I want to present and attribute the exhibit properly, so that it is credible and credited.
expected: Inline/drawer editable; rights carry credit + license; persists to library.json.

name: Studio keyboard shortcuts + cheat-sheet
story: As a curator, I want to drive common actions from the keyboard, so that I can work quickly and stay in flow.
expected: Shortcuts fire only when not typing in a field; ? shows grouped cheat-sheet; Esc unwinds layered UI.
edges: Space play vs form submit conflict
notes: src shortcuts.ts + ShortcutsHelp.svelte | GAP: shortcuts.ts dispatch unit test (no test exists)

name: Studio ingest media (file/folder/URL/IIIF)
story: As a curator, I want to bring my source material into an exhibit, so that I have something to annotate.
expected: File/folder dialog (native) or URL input; ingest strips junk, natural-sorts, bakes thumbnails.
edges: mixed image/AV folder groups by type
IIIF manifest -> one object per canvas
notes: native file dialog -> desktop/waived; URL+IIIF+sort logic testable via fs-seam. Seed opened.

name: Studio bind/save project (file/folder/browser-only)
story: As a curator, I want my work saved somewhere I trust with its save state visible, so that I never lose progress or doubt it's safe.
expected: Save dialog writes zip/folder; Cmd-S persists; unsaved dot shows; lost binding surfaced not silent.
notes: native fs/dialog -> desktop/waived; queue/binding logic testable via fs-seam. Seed opened.

name: Studio publish to GitHub Pages
story: As a curator, I want to put my exhibit online, so that others can read it.
expected: Dialog gathers owner/repo/branch/token (token never persisted); uploads media; pushes tree; flags broken links.
edges: broken intra-library links degrade to text
notes: GitHub API -> network lane; vendored stub, ECONNREFUSED=>flaky

name: Viewer object grid overview
story: As a reader, I want to size up an exhibit's contents at a glance, so that I can choose where to start.
expected: Grid shows objects with thumbnail, title, annotation count; single-object auto-collapses.
edges: broken image falls back to warm wash + name
notes: src ObjectGrid.svelte + MediaThumbnail.svelte | GAP: ObjectGrid.svelte render + count-badge component test | HUMAN-GATE Archie-79be: rendered-DOM/interaction, no e2e harness — data-logic covered by render-core, visual verdict deferred (not self-certified)

name: Viewer spatial image reader
story: As a reader, I want each observation tied to the part of the image it describes, so that I understand exactly what's being pointed at.
expected: Sidebar note list + legend + nav, OSD canvas; selection triggers fitBounds + mark highlight.
edges: object with no notes empty state

name: Viewer narrative prose-spine reader
story: As a reader, I want to be guided through the exhibit's argument, so that I can follow it without deciding what to look at next.
expected: Left prose panel; clicking section loads object + fits its media-fragment range; AV sections render player.

name: Viewer full-text finder overlay
story: As a reader, I want to find observations by what they say, so that I can reach what I care about quickly.
expected: Overlay with search + tag facet chips; minisearch fuzzy prefix; click arrives at note (reading+object+fitBounds).

name: Viewer reading legend overlay
story: As a reader, I want to switch between competing interpretations, so that I can consider each on its own terms.
expected: Legend shows General + named readings w/ swatches + per-object counts; selecting overlays that reading; hide-all toggles markers.
edges: hide-all on dense map keeps active reading

name: Viewer note media lightbox carousel
story: As a reader, I want to examine a note's images and media up close, so that I can study the evidence.
expected: Lightbox shows media + note text; counter; Esc/backdrop/x close; arrows step multi-media.
notes: src NoteLightbox.svelte | GAP: NoteLightbox.svelte carousel/arrow-nav component test | HUMAN-GATE Archie-79be: rendered-DOM/interaction, no e2e harness — data-logic covered by render-core, visual verdict deferred (not self-certified)

name: Viewer distraction-free reading sheet
story: As a reader, I want a calm, full view of a long note, so that I can read it without distraction.
expected: Fixed modal, 680px measure, scrolls long notes, renders prose cites as live links; Esc/backdrop close.

name: Viewer sidebar object stepper
story: As a reader, I want to move between an exhibit's objects, so that I can work through it at my own pace.
expected: Stepper shows X/N, prev/next disabled at boundaries, nav updates Reader without reload.
notes: src SidebarObjectNav.svelte | GAP: SidebarObjectNav.svelte boundary/disabled component test | HUMAN-GATE Archie-79be: rendered-DOM/interaction, no e2e harness — data-logic covered by render-core, visual verdict deferred (not self-certified)

name: Viewer resizable/collapsible sidebar
story: As a reader, I want to set how much space the notes versus the image take, so that the view suits how I'm reading.
expected: Drag resizes within clamp; collapse toggles; localStorage persists width+collapsed.

name: Viewer hover-to-solo note mark
story: As a reader, I want the note I'm considering to stand out, so that I'm not confused by the others.
expected: Hovered mark highlights, others desaturate; hover end restores; keyboard focus also solos.
notes: src Reader.svelte onnotehover | GAP: Reader.svelte onnotehover solo/desaturate component test | HUMAN-GATE Archie-79be: rendered-DOM/interaction, no e2e harness — data-logic covered by render-core, visual verdict deferred (not self-certified)

name: Viewer exhibit gallery landing
story: As a visitor, I want to browse the available exhibits, so that I can pick one to explore.
expected: Cards sorted by order with cover/fallback, title, description; click hash-routes to slug.

name: Viewer inline-cite hovercard
story: As a reader, I want to preview where a reference leads before following it, so that I can decide whether it's worth the detour.
expected: Card shows target kind/cover/title/breadcrumb + open affordance; click navigates in-app.
edges: moving onto card keeps it open

name: Viewer temporal AV reader + transcript
story: As a listener, I want the transcript and playback to move together, so that I can read along and jump to any moment.
expected: Transcript lines as t=start,end notes; click seeks; marker strip shows positions; activeNoteIndex tracks timeupdate.

name: Viewer open local .archie.zip
story: As an offline reader, I want to open an exhibit straight from a file, so that I can read it without internet access.
expected: File picker selects zip; exhibits load from portable tree; hash routing works; ?src= preloads hosted zip.
edges: invalid zip error in empty hall
notes: native file picker -> desktop/waived; zip-tree parse testable in browser. Seed opened.

name: Render remote IIIF tile fetch (CORS+timeout)
story: As a reader, I want images served from remote archives to load reliably, so that I can view material the exhibit doesn't host itself.
expected: OSD requests tiles Anonymous CORS; slow backends get 60s timeout; no canvas taint for WebGL.
notes: src mount.ts crossOriginPolicy/timeout; vendored fixture, timeout=>flaky

name: Render baked DZI pyramid tile load
story: As a reader, I want very large images to load progressively as I zoom, so that detail appears without waiting on the whole file.
expected: DZI descriptor parsed; OSD tile requests follow z/x/y pyramid; tiles load progressively.
notes: src mount.ts dziOsdSource + dzi.ts; vendored fixture

name: Render slippy XYZ map tiles
story: As a reader, I want maps to load as I pan and zoom, so that I can explore the geography behind a location note.
expected: OSD fetches z/x/y tiles; bounded map clamps pans; unbounded pans free.

name: Render fit-bounds sidebar reservation math
story: As a reader, I want a selected note to land where I can actually see it, so that the notes panel never hides what I clicked.
expected: fitBoundsRect grows bbox by w/(1-sidebarFraction) so note center lands in visible area (anvil oracle).
edges: note wider than viewport grows less (max 85%)

name: Render whole-object coverage border overlay
story: As a reader, I want a whole-object note to visibly frame the whole object, so that I can tell it isn't about one small region.
expected: SVG overlay at item bounds pans/zooms with object; corner hit-targets click-through; centre pointer-events none.
edges: image not open yet queues on open
notes: src frame-overlay.ts createFrameOverlay | GAP: frame-overlay.test.ts (createFrameOverlay has no test)

name: Render degenerate selector guard
story: As a reader, I want malformed regions skipped quietly, so that one bad annotation never breaks the whole view.
expected: isDegenerateSelectorValue detects NaN/empty/zero-size; bad selectors filtered before fitBounds.

name: Render zoom floor/ceiling band
story: As a reader, I want zoom kept within a readable range, so that I can't get lost zoomed too far in or out.
expected: zoomBand returns min/max from image/container aspect; OSD viewport constrained.

name: Render pan/scroll gesture guard
story: As a reader on a touch device, I want scrolling the notes to scroll the notes, so that I don't accidentally drag the image.
expected: Gesture guard separates list scroll vs canvas pan; multi-touch pinch zooms.

name: Render tile-source type resolution
story: As a reader, I want any kind of image source to just open, so that I don't have to care how it's hosted.
expected: resolveTileSource classifies image/IIIF-info/DZI/XYZ; mount wires OSD tile source.

name: Studio bulk-import annotations from CSV
story: As a curator, I want to bring annotations in from a spreadsheet, so that I can reuse work I've already organized elsewhere.
expected: CSV rows map to notes; x,y,w,h cells parse into regions via a strict state machine; partial or invalid regions error rather than silently drop.
edges: some-but-not-all region cells -> error (partial is malformed, not omitted)

name: Studio import W3C Web Annotation data
story: As a curator, I want to import standard Web Annotation data, so that I can build on annotations made in other tools.
expected: Tolerant of AnnotationPage / bare array / single Annotation; legacy sc:AnnotationList refused honestly; targets re-anchored by the /canvas/<id> tail.
edges: AnnotationPage vs bare array vs single Annotation
canvas id extracted from any publisher

name: Studio in-browser DZI tiling at publish
story: As a curator, I want very large images tiled when I publish, so that readers can deep-zoom them smoothly.
expected: dziTilePlan enumerates exactly the pyramid: one entry per (level,col,row) with {level}/{col}_{row}.{ext} path; no tiles dropped.
notes: Q-11 GO: web OffscreenCanvas DZI slicer

name: Studio contributor attribution
story: As a curator working with others, I want to see who wrote what, so that contributions are clear before I publish.
expected: collabBreakdown splits live notes by editor (deletions drop out) and sums across exhibits; summary names the other contributors, stays silent when solo.
notes: attribution logic tested; draft-banner COPY wording human-gated -> Archie-79be

name: Viewer shareable deep-link to a note
story: As a reader, I want to share a link that lands on the exact note I'm looking at, so that someone else sees what I see.
expected: IIIF Content State encodes/decodes an annotation reference losslessly; #/<slug>/a/<noteId> routes to the exhibit landing on that note; legacy ?annotation= redirects.

name: Studio export self-contained portable archive
story: As a curator, I want to save a self-contained copy of my exhibit, so that I can share or archive it offline.
expected: Publish builds a portable .archie.zip tree (assets + data bundled) that the viewer can open without a server.
edges: empty exhibit still a valid archive
notes: distinct publish target from GH Pages; bytes-building logic browser-testable
